title: About
content: Coral Futures Institute is a nonprofit marine conservation organization based in Kāne'ohe, Oahu, Hawaiʻi. Our science team leads long-term reef monitoring, active coral restoration, and community-based conservation programs across windward Oahu. This blog shares our field work, research findings, and advocacy efforts with the public.

title: Contact
content: Reach the Coral Futures Institute team at info@coralfuturesinstitute.org. For media inquiries contact Dr. Leilani Kamaka at l.kamaka@coralfuturesinstitute.org. We are located at the Kāne'ohe Marine Science Center, Kāne'ohe, Oahu.

title: Privacy Policy
content: This blog collects only the information you voluntarily submit through our contact form. We do not sell or share your information with third parties. Comment authors provide their name and email, which is stored securely and used only for moderation.

title: Our Research Team
content: Meet the scientists, coordinators, and community partners who make the Kāne'ohe Bay Coral Resilience Study possible. Led by Dr. Leilani Kamaka and Lorraine Roach, our team of 25 spans marine ecology, GIS, water quality science, and community engagement.

title: Research
content: Coral Futures Institute leads three active research programs on windward Oahu. The Kāne'ohe Bay Coral Resilience Study (Year 3) tracks long-term coral cover change across 14 monitoring stations. Our satellite-linked bleaching alert system delivers near-real-time thermal stress assessments. Our water quality monitoring network measures nutrient loading, turbidity, and pH at 12 sites around the bay.

title: Programs
content: Our programs connect science to community action. The Community Reef Monitoring Program trains local volunteers as certified reef observers. The Coral Nursery Program maintains 300+ fragment grow-out structures at an in-water nursery site in the bay. Our Education and Outreach Program delivers reef literacy content for schools, paddling clubs, and bay users.

title: Donate
content: Coral Futures Institute is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Donations fund field equipment, community training, and coral nursery operations. Every dollar helps us maintain science infrastructure that would otherwise depend on intermittent grant cycles. To donate, visit our secure giving page or contact Marisol Reyes at m.reyes@coralfuturesinstitute.org.

title: Volunteer
content: Join the Community Reef Monitoring Program as a trained observer. Two-day trainings are held quarterly and are open to anyone with basic swimming ability and a genuine interest in reef health. No scientific background required. The next training cohort runs in August 2026. Email n.kahale@coralfuturesinstitute.org to join the waitlist.

title: Resources
content: A curated list of resources for reef enthusiasts, educators, and policymakers: the NOAA CoralWatch bleaching alert portal, the DLNR Division of Aquatic Resources reef data portal, IUCN MPA guidelines, and CFI's publicly available transect data summaries. All CFI data releases are archived at info@coralfuturesinstitute.org on request.

title: Events
content: Upcoming events: Community Reef Monitoring (every first and third Saturday 9–12, Kāne'ohe Bay). Annual Fundraiser Gala — December 12, 2026, Seaside Pavilion, Kāne'ohe. Community Monitoring Network Expansion Launch — September 2026. All public events are listed here as they are confirmed.
